race.name: L'Etape Taiwan by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Etape Taiwan by Tour de France
race.tagline: 104km of Taiwanese lake paradise with 1,500m pain—Tour de France vibes in Asia!

vitals.location: Sun Moon Lake, Nantou County, Taiwan
vitals.field_size: 2,600 riders from global clubs in pro-style frenzy
vitals.registration: Entry delivers Tour de France jersey, finisher medal, pro support—costs TBD but worth every penny
vitals.prize_purse: Prestige over cash: Tour de France certification glory
vitals.aid_stations: Fully stocked pro-level stations mirroring TdF hydration heroes
distance_options: 29km: 18mi, 2,145ft — scenic lake shores via Ita Thao Pier

climate.description: Expect balmy 20-25°C mornings heating to sunny highs with lake breezes. Humidity adds grit but lush scenery distracts from sweat. Clear skies showcase emerald waters and peaks.

terrain.surface: Freshly paved highways like County Road 147 deliver glassy tarmac post-reconstruction. Winding descents and steady ascents mix smooth single-lanes with pro-grade quality. No gravel—just pure road bliss around emerald shores.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: Tour de France branding catapults this to T2 despite modest elevation—prestige overrides cap it firmly. Emerging Asian gem blends pro polish with exotic allure. Still building history but already punches above weight.

logistics.explanation: Taiwan's infrastructure shines—near Taichung hubs make access seamless. Closed roads scream pro event. Only gripe: international flights demand planning, but lake lodges seal the deal.

length.explanation: 104km hits solid gran fondo sweet spot without epic brutality. Perfect for suffering sans soul-crush. Shorties get 29km taste—smart inclusivity boosts appeal.

technicality.explanation: Winding descents and 12% kickers demand respect, but fresh pavement forgives. Tunnels add thrill sans sketch. Solid road test, not climber's nightmare.

elevation.explanation: 1,500m packs punch over 104km—steady 5% grind wears you down right. No mega-col, but lake returns sting sweet. Underrated for the views.

climate.explanation: Spring warmth teases tropical sweat-fests, humidity bites legs late. Breezes save the day amid peaks. Pack chamois cream—Taiwan heat builds character.

altitude.explanation: 700-1,500m feels lake-fresh, no hypoxia drama. Oxygen plentiful for power output. Pros: no acclimation needed; cons: zero high-alt bragging rights.

adventure.explanation: Exotic Taiwan lake, rice paddies, temples—pure escapism. TdF flair in Asia flips the script on Euro snoozefests. You'll Instagram harder than you pedal.

prestige.explanation: Official Tour de France L'Etape badge is gold—wear that jersey with pride. Global series elevates this beyond local grind. Instant cred for any palmares.

race_quality.explanation: Pro closed roads, 2,600 riders, flawless execution—feels like mini-TdF. Aid and vibes nail it. Young event, but punches like a veteran.

experience.explanation: Emerald lake, green hills, pro polish—sensory overload of beauty and burn. Finisher high rivals French Alps. Unforgettable Asia cycling baptism.

community.explanation: 2,600 international club riders foster electric buzz. Taiwanese hospitality warms the sufferfest. Global mix sparks stories over post-ride beers.

field_depth.explanation: Amateur-heavy with solid international turnout—no elites dominating. Competitive enough for KOM dreams. Room for mortals to shine.

value.explanation: TdF prestige at gran fondo price—steal for the production. Epic scenery free bonus. Delivers big on small investment.

expenses.explanation: Taiwan flights sting from West, but local cheap. Lodging/food steals. Worth the haul for uniqueness—budget wisely.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Etape Taiwan transplants Tour de France magic to Sun Moon Lake's stunning shores, blending 1,500m Taiwanese climbs with pro spectacle. Fresh pavement and global fields make it a must for adventure seekers tired of Alps reruns. Humidity tests grit, but emerald vistas and prestige jersey reward every watt. Emerging star that's already essential.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Bucket-list for prestige chasers. Nails the gran fondo formula with exotic twist. Race it before it blows up.

final_verdict.one_liner: TdF flair conquers Taiwan's lake paradise
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if craving prestige jersey and Asian vibes with manageable suffering. Skip if mega-elevation junkie or hate humidity.
final_verdict.alternatives: Taiwan KOM for brutal climbs, L'Etape Japan for nearby Asia TdF fix, Haute Route Alps for Euro purity

logistics.airport: Fly into Taichung (RMQ) 1hr away or Taipei (TPE) 3hrs—shuttles abound. Trains to Nantou seamless for bike bags.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Lake resorts book early—Xiangshan area prime. Mix camping for budget or luxe spas post-ride.
logistics.food: Night markets fuel carb feasts—stinky tofu to beef noodles. Event aid handles race day perfectly.
logistics.packet_pickup: Xiangshan Visitor Center day prior—smooth pro setup.
logistics.parking: Ample lakeside lots for event—car-free roads rule.

history.founder: Tour de France L'Etape Series organizers
history.origin_story: Launched 2024 as Asia's first L'Etape, transforming Sun Moon Lake's famed paths into TdF-certified haven. Blends Taiwanese tourism push with global cycling prestige. Second edition 2025 drew 2,600, cementing its rise.
notable_moments: Global clubs flock for Asian TdF debut
history.reputation: Rapid riser as world-class destination. TdF polish meets exotic beauty—buzz builds fast.

course_description.character: Starts fast downhill from Xiangshan into Puli flats, then unleashes KOM beast on Road 147. Winding lake returns via Road 21 grind steady pain amid emerald bliss. Pro closed roads amplify Tour intensity.

suffering_zones.label: KOM Challenge
suffering_zones.desc: 7km rampage hits 7-12% max—riders crack here under sun. Gazebo top offers false hope before descent. Pure leg-burner defines the day.

suffering_zones.label: Lake Return
suffering_zones.desc: 9km Route 21 slog at 4-6% wears weary legs home. Brief downhill teases before final push. Rewarding views mask accumulating fatigue.

course_description.signature_challenge: The Road 147 KOM: 7km of escalating pain peaking at 12%, freshly paved for max suffering. Tests resolve amid rice fields—true TdF suffering in Taiwan.

guide_variables.race_name: L'Etape Taiwan by Tour de France
guide_variables.race_location: Sun Moon Lake, Nantou County, Taiwan
guide_variables.race_weather: Warm humid spring with lake breezes

non_negotiables.why: Builds base for KOM grind and total distance. Humidity demands heat adaptation. Ensures cutoff crush without bonk.
Fast drops demand bike handling. Taiwan rains possible—practice saves seconds. Pro speeds require trust.

videos.title: Highlights | ☀️ L'Etape Sun Moon Lake by Tour de France | 2025 🚲
videos.channel: L'Etape Series by Tour de France
videos.description:
---------

L'Etape Series is a series of amateur cycling events designed to experience the Tour de France Legend. It is the only event where you can race and feel like a Tour de France champion, and where you can enjoy the Tour de France unique atmosphere and passion.

“The Tour de France is the world's greatest cycling race. Each year it is passionately followed by fans around the world so it has naturally given rise to L'Etape by Tour de France series”, says Christian Prudhomme, Tour de France director. 

The series has now expanded to more than 22 international locations including:

🇫🇷 France
🇧🇷 Brazil
🇧🇬 Bulgaria
🇨🇷 Costa Rica
🇨🇿 Czech Republic
🇩🇰 Denmark
🇪🇨 Ecuador
🇺🇸 United States
🇬🇷 Greece
🇦🇪 United Arab Emirates
🇮🇪 Ireland
🇮🇹 Italy
🇲🇽 Mexico
🇵🇱 Poland
🇷🇴 Romania
🇸🇰 Slovakia
🇸🇮 Slovenia
🇹🇼 Taiwan
🇹🇷 Turkey


Among these Tour de France official cycling events is of course L'Étape du Tour. With a peloton of 45 000 riders and 50 nationalities, it is Europe's biggest cycling event. Riding on the same routes and under the same conditions as Tour de France champions: this is the promise that L'Étape du Tour has kept since 1993.

Check out https://www.letapeseries.com for more information.

Join us on:
-Facebook 👉 https://www.facebook.com/letapeseries
-Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/letapeseries
-Tiktok 👉 https://www.tiktok.com/@letapeseries
